summary: omnius ships postinstall daemon that exfiltrates host info over HTTP
details: The package declares both `preinstall` and `postinstall` lifecycle hooks in package.json (with curl invocations) and ships dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs, which loads child_process, os, fs, and http, calls os.userInfo(), runs synchronous shell commands via execSync (including ping), and issues outbound http.request GETs. The combination of automatic execution on `npm install`, host/user identity collection (os.userInfo, ping-based reachability/identity probing), shell command execution, and outbound HTTP from a lifecycle-installed daemon is the canonical install-time host beacon / exfiltration shape. A sibling script dist/scripts/web_scrape.py adds further wget/ping/POST behavior. Lifecycle hooks of this kind expose the installer's machine identity and command output to a remote endpoint without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
